dataset.description: This dataset provides comprehensive agricultural data for Rajasthan, India from 2018 to 2019. It contains four tabless: Crop_Production_Data, Water_Usage_Data, Soil_Analysis_Data, and Crop_Price_Data. This dataset will be a valuable resource for researchers and data scientists working in the agricultural sector, enabling them to analyze and model various aspects of agricultural practices and market dynamics in Rajasthan.

columns.description: Geographical region within Rajasthan where agricultural transactions occur. Contains 10 unique districts like Kota and Jaipur
columns.name: District

columns.description: Agricultural product being traded. Includes 23 different crops such as Pomegranate and Maize
columns.name: Crop

columns.description: Specific regulated market (APMC Mandi) where crop transactions take place. 24 unique markets with Alwar APMC Mandi being most frequent
columns.name: Market

columns.description: Transaction date ranging from 2018-2019 in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. Contains 424 unique dates reflecting market fluctuations
columns.name: Date

columns.description: Prevailing market price per quintal (100kg) in Indian Rupees. Shows significant variation (mean ₹2499 ±296) across crops and markets
columns.name: Price (INR/quintal)

columns.description: Administrative region for agricultural activities. 10 districts including Nagaur (most frequent) and Udaipur
columns.name: District

columns.description: Cultivated plant species. 23 types including Citrus (most frequent) and Maize
columns.name: Crop

columns.description: Growing season - Kharif (monsoon crops) or Rabi (winter crops). Contains almost equal distribution of both seasons
columns.name: Season

columns.description: Cultivated land area ranging from 5,276-20,000 hectares with mean ~15,047ha
columns.name: Area (hectares)

columns.description: Agricultural output per hectare with average 37.6 quintals (±8.2) and range up to 60.4 quintals
columns.name: Yield (quintals)

columns.description: Total crop output ranging from 1,728-10,835 tons with mean ~5,663 tons. Derived from area and yield measurements
columns.name: Production (metric tons)

columns.description: Administrative region with Jodhpur being the most sampled (128 entries). 10 unique districts matching other datasets
columns.name: District

columns.description: Categorical classification including Black lava soil (most common), Saline, and Clay soils
columns.name: Soil Type

columns.description: Soil acidity/alkalinity measurement ranging from 5.89 (acidic) to 9.04 (alkaline) with mean 7.5
columns.name: pH Level

columns.description: Percentage of organic content in soil (0.16%-3.76%) indicating fertility levels
columns.name: Organic Matter (%)

columns.description: Available nitrogen levels (12-46 kg/ha) crucial for plant growth
columns.name: Nitrogen Content (kg/ha)

columns.description: Phosphorus concentration (5-36 kg/ha) affecting root development
columns.name: Phosphorus Content (kg/ha)

columns.description: Potassium levels (24-55 kg/ha) important for crop quality
columns.name: Potassium Content (kg/ha)

columns.description: Geographical region with Nagaur having highest entries (116). 10 districts matching other datasets
columns.name: District

columns.description: Cultivated plant requiring irrigation. Onion is most frequent (56 entries)
columns.name: Crop

columns.description: Water application technique - Sprinkler (most common), Tube Well, Drip, or Canal irrigation
columns.name: Irrigation Method

columns.description: Actual water usage ranging 4,674-18,631 L/ha with mean ~10,557 L/ha
columns.name: Water Consumption (liters/hectare)

columns.description: Water resources accessible for irrigation (4,505-18,489 L/ha) matching consumption patterns
columns.name: Water Availability (liters/hectare)

desc: This dataset provides comprehensive agricultural data for Rajasthan, India from 2018 to 2019. It contains four tabless: Crop_Production_Data, Water_Usage_Data, Soil_Analysis_Data, and Crop_Price_Data. This dataset will be a valuable resource for researchers and data scientists working in the agricultural sector, enabling them to analyze and model various aspects of agricultural practices and market dynamics in Rajasthan.
goal: Analyze the impact of different irrigation methods and water usage efficiency on crop yield, identify areas and crop types with the most efficient use of water resources, and provide recommendations for optimizing water management and sustainable agriculture practices.
name: Visual Analytics System agriculture_goal1_1742627320

views.components: Legend for district colors, toggle switch for consumption line visibility

effect.description: Highlight selected irrigation method across all views

visualization.description: Faceted box plots showing yield distributions by irrigation method with horizontal reference lines indicating average water consumption per method
Scatter plot matrix with regression lines showing water-yield relationships per crop type
Heatmap showing water efficiency ratio (yield per liter) with adjacent bar chart of top performers
Stacked bar chart showing preferred irrigation methods per soil type with error bars indicating yield ranges

data.reason: The SQL error suggests that the view is missing an expected column. If the temporal analysis is still required, re-add the date column conditionally. If not, remove this requirement
data.requirement: Re-add the date column to price data for temporal analysis if necessary, otherwise remove the requirement

data.reason: The error indicates a specific incorrect column name or reference, so it's crucial to verify that all fields used in the queries are correctly spelled and available in the respective tables
data.requirement: Re-check the SQL queries for any incorrect field names or syntax issues

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW vw_water_production AS SELECT wp.Irrigation_Method, cp.Yield, wp.Water_Consumption, wp.District, wp.Crop FROM water_usage_data wp JOIN crop_production_data cp ON wp.Crop = cp.Crop AND wp.District = cp.District;
sqls.name: vw_water_production

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW vw_soil_analysis AS SELECT sa.District, sa.Soil_Type, sa.pH_Level, sa.Organic_Matter, sa.Nitrogen_Content, sa.Phosphorus_Content, sa.Potassium_Content FROM soil_analysis_data sa JOIN vw_water_production pw ON sa.District = pw.District;
sqls.name: vw_soil_analysis

tasks.method: Faceted box plots showing yield distributions grouped by irrigation method, with superimposed mean water consumption values using horizontal reference lines. Encoding: X-axis=Irrigation Method (categorical), Y-axis=Yield (quantitative), Color=District (categorical), Reference line=Avg water consumption (secondary Y-axis)
tasks.objective: Compare yield performance and water usage patterns across different irrigation methods

tasks.method: Scatter plot matrix with yield vs water consumption per crop type, enhanced with regression lines and R² values. Encoding: X=Water Consumption (quantitative), Y=Yield (quantitative), Color=Crop (categorical), Facet=Crop (columns)
tasks.objective: Identify water-use efficiency patterns across different crop types

tasks.method: Heatmap showing water efficiency (Yield/Water Consumption ratio) across districts. Encoding: X-axis=District (categorical), Y-axis=Crop (categorical), Color=Water Efficiency (quantitative). Linked bar chart shows top 10 district-crop combinations
tasks.objective: Locate highest water efficiency zones and their agricultural characteristics

tasks.method: Stacked bar chart showing irrigation method distribution per soil type with overlaid yield ranges. Encoding: X-axis=Soil Type (categorical), Y-axis=Count (quantitative), Stack=Irrigation Method (categorical), Overlay=Yield range (error bars)
tasks.objective: Reveal optimal irrigation-soil combinations for yield maximization

tasks.method: Deviation plot showing (Consumption - Availability) differences per crop. Encoding: X-axis=Crop (categorical), Y-axis=Water Deficit/Surplus (quantitative), Color intensity=Absolute deviation magnitude
tasks.objective: Identify mismatch between water availability and actual consumption patterns

tasks.method: Small multiples of grouped box plots showing NPK distribution across irrigation methods. Encoding: X-axis=Irrigation Method (categorical), Y-axis=Nutrient Level (quantitative), Facet=Nutrient Type (columns)
tasks.objective: Understand relationships between irrigation practices and soil nutrient preservation
